complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Setup Project Repository and Development Environment
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the setup of a new project repository and development environment into subtasks covering repository initialization, branching strategy, CI/CD configuration, coding standards documentation, and developer environment setup.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: This task involves standard but essential setup steps. While each step is well-understood in the industry, coordination and ensuring consistency across the team adds moderate complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Decompose the development of a core vehicle physics engine into subtasks for mass and inertia modeling, torque and power delivery, suspension geometry, wheel physics, drivetrain simulation, vehicle configuration support, and integration testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Developing a realistic vehicle physics engine is highly complex, requiring deep domain knowledge, mathematical modeling, and robust testing to ensure accurate and performant simulation.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: List subtasks for procedural terrain generation, including heightmap generation, noise layering, terrain type support, difficulty scaling, feature placement, and system integration.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Procedural terrain generation with multiple terrain types and scalability introduces significant algorithmic and implementation challenges, especially for natural appearance and performance.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the advanced suspension system into subtasks for multi-joint simulation, component modeling (control arms, springs, shocks), compression/rebound logic, articulation, support for multiple suspension types, and testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Simulating detailed suspension systems with realistic articulation and load handling is technically demanding, requiring accurate physical modeling and support for various configurations.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Identify subtasks for implementing dynamic terrain deformation, including mesh deformation, displacement mapping, erosion simulation, compression effects, persistence, and multiplayer synchronization.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Dynamic terrain deformation is complex due to real-time mesh manipulation, visual fidelity, and the need for consistent multiplayer synchronization.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Divide the collision detection system into subtasks for broad-phase detection, narrow-phase detection, collision shape creation, spatial partitioning, collision response, performance optimization, and integration testing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Robust collision detection is critical and challenging, especially with complex vehicle and terrain interactions, requiring efficient algorithms and high accuracy.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the winch system into subtasks for cable physics, tension and elasticity simulation, attachment logic, multi-point support, and configuration options.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: A realistic winch system involves intricate physics and attachment logic, but is more contained in scope compared to core physics or collision systems.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Tire Deformation and Grip System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: List subtasks for tire deformation and grip, including sidewall flex simulation, tread deformation, air pressure modeling, grip coefficient calculation, terrain interaction, and support for multiple tire types.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Simulating tire deformation and variable grip is technically challenging, requiring real-time physics and integration with terrain and vehicle systems.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Decompose the rendering pipeline setup into subtasks for PBR material support, dynamic lighting, shadow implementation, post-processing effects, rendering optimizations, and shader framework setup.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Implementing a modern rendering pipeline with advanced effects and optimizations is complex, involving graphics programming and performance considerations.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the adaptive camera system into subtasks for multiple camera presets, obstacle avoidance, smooth transitions, per-wheel camera angles, and camera controls.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: A versatile camera system requires careful design for usability and smooth operation, but leverages established patterns in game development.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Create Water and Mud Physics System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: List subtasks for water and mud physics, including fluid dynamics, buoyancy and drag, mud viscosity simulation, displacement effects, vehicle interaction, and visual effects implementation.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Simulating water and mud with realistic physics and visuals is complex, requiring integration of fluid dynamics and terrain interaction.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Basic HUD and User Interface
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the UI implementation into subtasks for HUD design, status indicators, navigation markers, menu creation, and debug visualization tools.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: While UI systems are standard, integrating real-time vehicle data and modularity adds moderate complexity.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement Audio System with Engine Sounds
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: List subtasks for the audio system, including 3D audio engine setup, engine sound modeling, environmental audio, sound effects for interactions, and audio mixing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Realistic audio systems require careful sound modeling and integration, but follow established patterns in game development.

complexityAnalysis.taskTitle: Implement CB Radio and Communication System
complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the CB radio system into subtasks for channel selection, audio effects, distance-based degradation, team/global channels, and music broadcasting.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Implementing in-game communication with realistic effects and channel management is moderately complex, especially with audio streaming.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: List subtasks for in-game radio, including station creation, playlist management, DJ role assignment, broadcasting controls, and dynamic volume mixing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Music and radio systems are well-understood, but require integration with audio and UI systems for seamless user experience.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down free roam mode into subtasks for single/multiplayer support, dynamic event system, challenge generation, player progress tracking, team drive implementation, and system integration.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Free roam modes involve complex state management, multiplayer support, and dynamic content generation.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: List subtasks for competitive modes, including mode design, scoring systems, timers, victory conditions, and integration with core gameplay systems.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Competitive modes require additional logic for objectives and scoring, but build on existing gameplay systems.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the mission system into subtasks for mission framework, challenge creation, objective tracking, reward system, and event scheduling.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Mission systems require flexible frameworks and integration with progression and event systems, adding moderate complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Decompose multiplayer functionality into subtasks for networking architecture, vehicle synchronization, lag compensation, team formation, player interaction systems, recovery/winch integration, and resource sharing.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Core multiplayer systems are highly complex, requiring robust networking, real-time synchronization, and support for diverse interactions.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: List subtasks for vehicle customization, including performance modifications, visual customization, equipment loadouts, parts system, and visual representation integration.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Customization systems require flexible data structures and UI integration, but follow established patterns in game development.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down the parts store into subtasks for store interface, part categorization, compatibility checking, purchasing system, and token economy integration.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: In-game stores are standard but require careful integration with customization and economy systems.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: List subtasks for the vehicle marketplace, including listing creation, token-based transactions, rating/review system, featured vehicles curation, and search/filter functionality.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Player-to-player marketplaces add complexity with user-generated content, transactions, and moderation.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down progression and economy into subtasks for ranking system, experience tracking, token earning/spending, team reputation, and secret features/easter eggs.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Progression and economy systems require careful balance and integration, but are well-understood in game development.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: List subtasks for social/community features, including team management, statistics/achievements, trail rating, vehicle showcase, chat system, and social media integration.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Community features require integration with backend systems and external APIs, adding moderate complexity.

complexityAnalysis.expansionPrompt: Break down spectator and replay system into subtasks for live spectating, camera controls, director mode, broadcasting tools, session recording, and replay playback/highlights.
complexityAnalysis.reasoning: Spectator and replay systems are complex due to real-time data capture, playback accuracy, and user controls.
